# Incursa.Quic

> Automation bootstrap for the `Incursa.Quic` repository. This file is non-authoritative.

When working in this repository:

- Follow this authority order: `specs/` landing pages and the gap tracker, [`docs/requirements-workflow.md`](./docs/requirements-workflow.md), root docs, [`AGENTS.md`](./AGENTS.md), then this file.
- Canonical SpecTrace artifacts are authored in sibling `.json` files under [`specs/`](./specs/README.md).
- Record unresolved protocol questions in [`specs/requirements/quic/REQUIREMENT-GAPS.md`](./specs/requirements/quic/REQUIREMENT-GAPS.md) before implementation.
- Keep requirements, architecture, work items, verification, tests, fuzzing, and benchmarks separate.
- For parsing, serialization, encoding, decoding, or other wire-facing code, expect positive tests, negative tests, fuzz/property coverage, and benchmarks.
- Use repository-relative links for repo-local Markdown.
- Do not infer missing protocol rules into code before the requirement slice exists.
- Before finishing a trace-surface change, run `pwsh -NoProfile -File scripts/Validate-SpecTraceJson.ps1 -Profiles core` and `dotnet tool run workbench -- --format json validate --profile core`.

## Useful Starting Points

- [`README.md`](./README.md)
- [`docs/README.md`](./docs/README.md)
- [`docs/requirements-workflow.md`](./docs/requirements-workflow.md)
- [`docs/testing/README.md`](./docs/testing/README.md)
- [`specs/README.md`](./specs/README.md)
- [`specs/requirements/quic/README.md`](./specs/requirements/quic/README.md)
